title: PLAN Type 055 destroyer leads task group exercises east of Luzon Strait in Balikatan response
summary: The PLA Navy's Type 055 guided missile destroyer — China's most capable surface combatant — led a four-ship task group conducting exercises east of the Luzon Strait, explicitly linked by the Southern Theater Command to the ongoing Balikatan 2026 drills.
contents:
## Type 055 Destroyer Leads PLAN Task Group Near Philippines

### Force Composition
The PLA Southern Theater Command deployed a surface task group east of the Luzon Strait consisting of one Type 055 guided missile destroyer (112 VLS cells, dual-band radar), one Type 052D guided missile destroyer, one Type 054A guided missile frigate, and one Type 903A auxiliary oil replenishment vessel. The Type 055 represents China's most advanced surface combatant.

### Carrier Activity
The PLAN aircraft carrier Liaoning transited the Taiwan Strait southbound on April 20. Unverified satellite imagery circulating on Chinese social media purports to show Liaoning operating in the South China Sea accompanied by three guided missile destroyers and six guided missile frigates — a significant task group deployment.

### Equipment Significance
The Type 055's presence signals Beijing's willingness to commit premier assets to counter-demonstrations against multinational exercises. The vessel's 112 vertical launch system cells can accommodate a mix of anti-ship, anti-air, and land-attack cruise missiles, giving it significant multi-mission capability.

### Type 076 Sea Trials
Global Times reported the PLAN's new Type 076 landing helicopter dock departed Shanghai for sea trials in the South China Sea, potentially supporting the broader naval response to Balikatan. The Type 076 represents China's first vessel designed to operate fixed-wing aircraft from a flat deck without a ski-jump.

**Source:** ISW-AEI, Global Times
